purpose: Help a design partner evaluate whether SMERC is worth a controlled shadow-mode pilot.

criteria.question: Can AI agents, copilots, scripts, or automations propose or trigger meaningful side effects?
Are GitHub Actions, CI/CD, infrastructure workflows, security automation, or agent tool calls in scope?
Are existing approval controls difficult to replay or explain after the fact?
Are simple allow/block decisions too blunt for some workflows?
Are rollback, containment, or blast radius recurring concerns?
Can reviewers spend time calibrating SMERC decisions during shadow mode?

review_areas.title: Action contract
checks: Proposed actions can be expressed as smerc.action.v1.
Action metadata avoids secrets, credentials, customer PII, and unnecessary source-code contents.
Action hashes are deterministic for replay.
Posture outputs include reason codes and controls.

review_areas.title: Runtime decision behavior
checks: Representative actions can be evaluated locally.
High-reversibility actions do not receive unnecessary blocking.
High-exposure, low-recovery actions do not receive ALLOW.
Reason codes are useful to reviewers.

review_areas.title: API and audit
checks: Authentication is enabled for shared environments.
Idempotency is used for workflow retries.
Replay IDs and decision records are tenant-scoped and retrievable.
Reviews are immutable and denominator-aware metrics are available.

review_areas.title: Permit and evidence path
checks: Eligible enforcement decisions can issue action-bound permits.
Permit preparation occurs before native controls run.
Signed control evidence is required for configured executor audiences.
Permit consumption is atomic and single use.

review_areas.title: GitHub deployment adapter
checks: Execution plans validate in validate mode.
The adapter uses argument arrays rather than shell strings.
Report artifacts exclude raw command output and secrets.
Timeout, cancellation, and rollback behavior are understood.
